title: AgentOS wind.json — desktop window-drag → wind signal
description: Sibling of agent.json in $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/nimbus-aurora/. Written atomically by the agentosd 'wind' sink (dot-prefixed temp + rename, exactly like feed.rs::write_feed). The shader consumer polls it (a Timer-driven poller, NOT sync-XHR — see hills-reactive README) and maps windDir = f(dir), gust = g(gust). EASED/LOW-PASSED state is what the sink writes: the producer (KWin script) emits raw deltas; the sink applies the calm spring (spec §3). Idle / no producer / stale → neutral (gust 0, dir [0,-1]); the shader resolves to its resting motion (idle byte-identical, spec §5).

schema.description: Versioned per the ADR-0009 two-consumer lesson. Bump on any breaking shape change; the consumer pins it.

dir.description: Eased UNIT direction vector in screen space (x right+, y down+, Wayland axis). Magnitude ~1 while a drag is active; held last-good and decayed toward the neutral [0,-1] as gust eases to 0. The shader maps this to its own up-axis (a deterministic consumer-side constant).

gust.description: Eased gust strength. A drag NUDGES this up (toward the drag's normalized px/s speed) and the spring eases it back toward 0. Bounded so a fast flick can't blow out the wind.

active.description: True while a drag is in progress (producer-reported). False the moment the drag ends OR when the signal goes stale (sink-decayed). Lets the consumer distinguish a live shove from the gentle ease-back.

updated_at.description: Wall-clock epoch seconds when the sink last wrote. The consumer treats a wind.json older than STALE_SECS as neutral (fail-open). The sink itself decays gust to 0 and writes a final neutral frame after a drag, so a present-but-old file already reads calm.
